title: Oops!... Pakistan did it again: The 'deny, deflect, get caught' pattern repeats
content: Pakistan's history of 'oops' moments, from allegedly harboring Iranian aircraft to denying terrorist camps, has eroded its credibility on the global stage. Decades of strategic ambiguity and carefully calibrated deniability have created a trust deficit, making its diplomatic assurances met with skepticism and every crisis a potential international embarrassment.

title: Trump's 'CEO crew' to Beijing is almost as rich as world's third-largest economy
content: Donald Trump has landed in Beijing with 17 American companies in tow,   worth a combined $16.47 trillion—roughly 90% of China's entire 2025 GDP. Jensen Huang joined at the last minute, boarding Air Force One in Anchorage. Nvidia and Apple alone account for $9.7 trillion of that, while Meta sent its policy chief after Beijing blocked its $2 billion Manus deal.

title: Patience as power: How Beijing turned America's Iran war into a Taiwan strategy
content: While the US and Israel engaged in conflict with Iran, China quietly leveraged the situation to expand its influence. By positioning itself as a diplomatic alternative and stabilizing energy supplies, Beijing benefited from a weakened American strategic position and growing uncertainty about US reliability among allies.

title: 'It is right thing': Ex-Army chief backs RSS gen secy Hosabale on dialogue with Pak
content: Former Army chief General (retired) Manoj Mukund Naravane has endorsed Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) general secretary Dattatreya Hosabale's call for increased people-to-people contact between India and Pakistan. Naravane believes stronger citizen ties can improve bilateral relations between the two countries, stating common people share similar concerns and have no stake in politics. Hosabale is the RSS number two after Sangh chief Mohan Bhagwat.

title: Walmart to combine global tech & product teams; to lay off or relocate 1,000 employees
content: Walmart is restructuring its global technology and product teams, leading to approximately 1,000 corporate employees facing layoffs or relocation. This move aims to improve efficiency by combining similar functions and aligning organizational efforts. The retail giant is also experiencing a slowdown in tech hiring, reflected in a significant reduction of H-1B visa applications.

title: ‘We did not fire Sreejesh’: Hockey India responds after legend's explosive allegation
content: Hockey India clarified that legendary goalkeeper PR Sreejesh's tenure as junior men's coach concluded as per contract, denying claims of him being "fired." The federation stated his position was advertised and a new coach selected on merit. An alternative development role was offered but declined. Hockey India emphasised its commitment to developing Indian coaches.

title: 'AIADMK has been declining': CV Shanmugam targets EPS after removal from party posts
content: AIADMK leader CV Shanmugam criticized party chief Edappadi K Palaniswami, alleging continuous electoral setbacks since he assumed leadership after Jayalalithaa's death. Shanmugam highlighted that while the party faced defeats before, Jayalalithaa had the capability to bring it back to power, a feat he claims EPS has failed to achieve, leading to worsening electoral performance.

title: Iran war could become a trillion-dollar burden for US
content: Washington's campaign against Iran has surged to nearly $29 billion, surpassing initial Pentagon estimates. Experts warn the escalating costs, driven by operational and equipment replacement, could ultimately reach a staggering $1 trillion.  This financial burden is becoming a political flashpoint ahead of US elections, with concerns over inflation.

title: Empty offices, locked buildings & fake work location: US agency flags OPT visa fraud
content: US immigration agencies have uncovered widespread fraud within the Optional Practical Training (OPT) program, involving thousands of foreign students and suspect employers. Investigations revealed fake offices, empty buildings, and companies claiming to employ hundreds with little to no actual business activity, suggesting a "guestworker pipeline" issue.

title: IPL: Why Varun Chakaravarthy is not playing in KKR’s crucial clash vs RCB
content: Kolkata Knight Riders face a significant blow as star spinner Varun Chakaravarthy is ruled out of their IPL 2026 clash against Royal Challengers Bengaluru due to an injury sustained in the previous match. Uncapped pacer Saurabh Dubey makes his IPL debut as KKR navigates this crucial encounter without their key bowler, who has been instrumental in their recent resurgence.

title: 'Don't you understand extent of our victory?' Vijay lambasts DMK after floor test win
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay's government won a trust vote with 144 MLAs supporting it. Following the victory, Vijay penned an open letter to the public, criticizing the opposition DMK for attempting to undermine his administration. He highlighted his party's electoral performance and commitment to secular values, while accusing the DMK of hypocrisy in coalition politics.

title: AIADMK crisis deepens: EPS removes rebel leaders backing CM Vijay
content: AIADMK's internal conflict escalated as party chief Palaniswami expelled the rebel leaders. These leaders had backed Tamil Nadu CM Vijay during a crucial trust vote on the assembly floor. The move follows accusations of weakening the party's anti-DMK stance and exploring alliances. This leadership struggle intensifies ahead of future elections.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sold their electrical co for $1.7bn and gave 540 workers $240 m of it
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: Sanjay Gupta on Rs 300 crore ‘Dhurandhar’ gamble with Ranveer Singh
content: Filmmaker Sanjay Gupta praised Aditya Dhar's vision behind 'Dhurandhar', calling it a massive risk given Ranveer Singh's career low and Dhar's limited filmmaking experience. He credited the Ambanis' backing as a key factor in the film's success. The two-part franchise has reportedly crossed Rs 3000 crore worldwide.

title: ISL: 'If this deadlock is not resolved, multiple clubs will now shut down'
content: Indian Super League (ISL) clubs face an uncertain future due to a deadlock with the AIFF over commercial rights. Clubs are pushing for a collaborative operational model, citing financial woes and a lack of clarity that threatens their continued participation and the league's ecosystem.

title: Ryan Johnson linked to Canucks GM role after Evan Gold hiring rumours
content: The Vancouver Canucks may have changed their approach in the hunt for a new general manager after initial reports had linked Evan Gold to the opening. With the latest NHL updates, Ryan Johnson is now the front-runner for the job after his success with the Abbotsford Canucks. The shake-up in Vancouver’s front office could also mean greater leadership roles for Daniel Sedin and Henrik Sedin.

title: Alexander Zverev denied entry at Rome restaurant before Italian Open loss
content: Alexander Zverev endured a difficult 24 hours in Rome after being denied entry to a famous restaurant and then crashing out of the Italian Open. The World No. 3 was recently turned away from the popular Japanese restaurant Zuma in central Rome because he did not have a reservation. Reports also claimed that the restaurant staff failed to recognise the German tennis star when he arrived with his brother Mischa Zverev and a friend for dinner.

title: Ben Shelton and Trinity Rodman’s net worth and endorsement fortune
content: American tennis star Ben Shelton and U.S. soccer forward Trinity Rodman have become one of the most talked-about young sports couples in recent years. Both athletes are enjoying rapid success in their respective sports while building impressive fortunes at a young age. Shelton has become one of the rising faces of men’s tennis, while Rodman continues to shine for the Washington Spirit and the United States women’s national soccer team.

title: Air India suspends, reduces frequency on 29 international routes till Aug - full list
content: Air India is suspending and reducing flights on 29 international routes between June and August 2026 due to the West Asia conflict, rising jet fuel prices, and airspace restrictions. These temporary changes aim to improve operational stability and mitigate passenger inconvenience amid global aviation disruptions. The airline will continue operating over 1,200 international flights monthly across five continents.

title: IPL: What happens if RCB vs KKR is washed out in Raipur? Playoff race explained
content: Rain in Raipur threatens the IPL 2026 clash between Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Kolkata Knight Riders, potentially impacting playoff hopes. A washout would significantly boost RCB's qualification chances, keeping them second. For KKR, it's a damaging blow, pushing them to seventh and demanding wins in all remaining games after an earlier tied contest.

title: Pakistan honours Shahid Afridi with Hilal-e-Imtiaz, fourth cricketer to receive it
content: Former Pakistan captain Shahid Afridi has been honored with the Hilal-e-Imtiaz, Pakistan's second-highest civilian award, for his significant contributions to cricket and his extensive service to the national team. This prestigious recognition marks him as the fourth cricketer to receive this honour, celebrating his impactful white-ball career and global franchise presence.

title: Telugu actor Rajeev Kanakala clarifies controversial school event remarks
content: Actor Rajeev Kanakala clarified his presence at a controversial school event, stating his sole intention was to motivate students and interact with parents. He admitted to being unaware of allegations against the school prior to attending. Kanakala advised parents to verify school credentials independently and requested organizers not to use his image for promotion, earning praise for his transparent approach.

title: ‘Removed for foreign coach’: Sreejesh slams HI, asks: ‘Can’t Indians develop hockey?’
content: Hockey legend PR Sreejesh expressed shock after his abrupt removal as junior men's coach, despite leading the team to five podium finishes in five tournaments, including a World Cup bronze. He revealed Hockey India's preference for foreign coaches, citing the senior team's chief coach's recommendation, questioning the development of Indian talent.

title: Blood clot in lungs caused Prateek Yadav’s death, reveals post-mortem report
content: In a tragic turn of events, Prateek Yadav, the son of the late Mulayam Singh Yadav, succumbed to a severe blood clot that obstructed his lungs, resulting in heart and respiratory failure. Autopsy reports reveal that he experienced a 'cardiorespiratory collapse due to massive pulmonary thromboembolism.

title: 'I don’t know how Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will last': AB de Villiers raises alarm
content: AB de Villiers lauded Rajasthan Royals' 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's white-ball prowess, but cautioned that his long-term success hinges on adapting to multi-format cricket. While Sooryavanshi's IPL and U19 numbers are exceptional, de Villiers stressed the need for exposure to ODIs and Tests to truly test his mettle and uncover new layers of his game.

title: NEET UG paper leak case: CBI arrests five accused, raids multiple locations
content: The CBI has arrested five individuals and launched nationwide searches in connection with alleged irregularities and a paper leak for the NEET UG-2026 examination. The investigation follows a complaint from the Union education ministry, with charges including criminal conspiracy and cheating. The NTA had previously cancelled the exam, affecting nearly 23 lakh candidates, due to suspected circulation of leaked questions.

title: Mike Vrabel praised by Patriots as Dianna Russini faces career turmoil
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach’s life has remain unchanged as he has resumed his professional duties amid his alleged affair drama with Dianna Russini, sparking chaos among millions. While the outrage against Dianna Russini was so strong that she had to step down from her prestigious role at The Athletic, it seems Mike Vrabel continues to be a favorite one in the team.

title: CBSE Class 12th result: Why students scored lower this year as pass percentage falls
content: CBSE declared the Class XII results on May 13, 2026, with an overall pass percentage of 85.20%, down from 88.39% last year. Over 15 lakh students cleared the examination out of 17.68 lakh who appeared. The decline of 3.19 percentage points coincided with the introduction of On-Screen Marking (OSM) for evaluation and reportedly tougher Physics and Mathematics papers. Experts cited multiple factors, including digital assessment and competency-based questions under NEP.

title: Mouni Roy, Suraj Nambiar split six months ago, divorce yet to be filed: Report
content: Mouni Roy and Suraj Nambiar's marriage is reportedly over, with sources revealing they separated six months ago. While divorce hasn't been filed, Mouni has consulted lawyers.  Speculation arose after they unfollowed each other on Instagram and Mouni removed couple photos.  Reports suggest Suraj felt overshadowed by Mouni's fame.

title: Cruise ship with 1,700 passengers onboard isolated after suspected norovirus death
content: Over 1,700 people are confined to a cruise ship in Bordeaux after a 90-year-old passenger died from a suspected norovirus infection. Approximately 50 others onboard are exhibiting symptoms of the highly contagious stomach bug. Health officials are implementing strict measures to prevent further spread of the virus.

title: Virat Kohli’s strong record against KKR faces toughest challenge in Sunil Narine
content: Virat Kohli, IPL's leading run-scorer, faces Kolkata Knight Riders in IPL 2026. Despite a strong season, he seeks to break a rare duck streak. Kohli boasts an impressive record against KKR, but faces a significant challenge from Sunil Narine, who has historically contained him.

title: 'EPS has no such idea': Rebel AIADMK MLA lobs 'betrayer' charge at party chief
content: AIADMK faces a severe internal crisis as a faction of MLAs, led by C.V. Shanmugam, openly rebelled against party chief Edappadi Palaniswami. They allege EPS explored aligning with the DMK and abandoned the party's core anti-DMK stance.

title: Hardik Pandya Update: After over 3 hrs training, will he play PBKS match?
content: Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya's intense training session fuels speculation amid his absence due to back spasms. While the team cites recovery, his vigorous workout raises questions about his availability. Pandya and stand-in skipper Suryakumar Yadav are currently away from the squad, impacting the team's forgettable season where they have already been eliminated from playoff race.

title: The US has lost the reputation war — to Russia and China
content: A new global survey reveals a significant drop in America's international image under President Trump, now trailing behind Russia and China.  Concerns over his foreign policy, tariffs, and military actions have unsettled allies and created global unease.  This decline marks the second consecutive year of falling perceptions for the US.

title: Assam cabinet clears draft UCC Bill, to be tabled in Assembly on May 26
content: Assam's cabinet has approved the Uniform Civil Code Bill, slated for introduction in the Assembly on May 26. This move aligns with the BJP's commitment to a common set of civil laws for marriage, divorce, and inheritance, aiming for legal uniformity across communities. The state government emphasises prioritising Assam's identity and development agenda while enacting this significant legislation.

title: Mike Vrabel’s marriage rocked after Dianna Russini clip resurfaces online
content: A resurfaced 2020 video featuring Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini has reignited online speculation surrounding their alleged relationship. The clip shows Russini giving intimacy advice during a Barstool segment while Vrabel jokingly responds, “I’m paying very close attention… I’m so excited.” The renewed attention comes after recent photos and videos of the pair went viral. Although no divorce or affair has been officially confirmed, the controversy continues fueling intense discussion across NFL and social media circles.

title: ISL clubs propose club-led model for operations, commercialisation
content: Indian Super League clubs, excluding East Bengal, have proposed a club-led model for future league operations and commercialisation. They aim to manage responsibilities, citing local market knowledge and cost-saving potential. This proposal comes ahead of a crucial meeting with the AIFF president Kalyan Chaubey on May 22.

title: Trains at 220 kmph on Indian Railways soon! Cabinet approves project; check details
content: The proposed rail line is expected to improve connectivity between Ahmedabad, Dholera Special Investment Region, the upcoming Dholera International Airport and the Lothal National Maritime Heritage Complex.

title: The Siraj-Rabada powerplay blueprint: Test-match lengths, T20 destruction
content: Mohammad Siraj and Kagiso Rabada have become IPL 2026's most lethal new-ball pair, bowling the entire powerplay in five consecutive games. Their relentless 'Test-match length' bowling, consistently hitting good and back-of-length areas, ripped through SRH's top order, claiming 4 wickets within the first 6 overs. This bowling prowess has been the driving force behind Gujarat Titans' success this season.

title: Amid NEET Row, Vijay Says "Abolish" Exam, Admit Students With Class 12 Marks
content: In his statement posted on social media platform X today, Vijay pointed out that this was not the first time NEET has been compromised.

title: Prateek Yadav Had 6 Injuries Before Death, Clot In Lung Killed Him: Autopsy
content: Prateek Yadav's autopsy report mentioned injuries to his chest, under his right arm, right forearm, right elbow joint, over the elbow and left wrist.

title: Parts Of Delhi See Light Rain, Orange Alert Issued For Thunderstorms, Hail
content: The warning was forwarded to the Delhi State Disaster Management Authority (SDMA), with authorities advising residents to follow safety guidelines during the thunderstorm activity.

title: 'Don't Have Control Over Our Enriched Uranium': Iran Minister
content: Kazem Gharibabadi, Deputy Foreign Minister of Iran, in an exclusive interview with NDTV, added that Iran had been ready to discuss all aspects of the nuclear issue but that the US had refused genuine negotiations and instead demanded its own terms.

title: Mumbai Airport Strengthens Green Push With Top Recognition
content: From decarbonisation to zero waste, one of the world's busiest single airport runways is making strides in adopting sustainable practices.

title: Amul Raises Milk Prices By Rs 2 Per Litre Across India, First Hike Since 2025
content: According to the GCMMF, the revision is necessitated by a rise in the overall cost of operations and milk production.

title: Amul Hikes Milk Prices By Rs 2 Per Litre Across Major Variants In India
content: GCMMF, in a statement, said it has "increased the prices of fresh pouch milk by Rs 2 per litre in major milk selling variants/packs across India effective from May 14".

title: Live Updates: Kerala Chief Minister Announcement Tomorrow, Says Congress
content: Kerala Chief Minister Live Updates: A formal announcement is expected today, once Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge returns to Delhi and holds further talks with senior figures, including Sonia Gandhi.

title: Iran Says Control Over Hormuz To Bring "Significant" Economic Revenues
content: Iran's military spokesman said on Wednesday that Tehran's control over the Strait of Hormuz could generate "significant" economic revenue and strengthen the country's international position.

title: Behind Marco Rubio Getting Into China, A Change In How His Name Is Written
content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio was banned from entering China, so, finding a diplomatic workaround, the Chinese government and official media transliterated the first syllable of his surname with a different Chinese character for "lu".

title: Auto Driver, 65, Dies After E-Scooter Explodes Inside Bengaluru Home
content: A 65-year-old auto driver died after an electric two-wheeler allegedly exploded while being charged inside a house in Bengaluru's Murphy Town area under the jurisdiction of the Halasuru Police Station.

title: Tamil Nadu Government Floor Test LIVE Updates: EPS Sacks CV Shanmugam From AIADMK Post For Voting In Vijay's Favour
content: Tamil Nadu Government Floor Test LIVE Updates: A rebel faction of the AIADMK, comprising 30 MLAs, said they will support Vijay in Wednesday's floor test.

title: Cabinet Approves Increase In MSP For 14 Kharif Crops For 2026-27
content: The government said the revised MSP is in line with the Union Budget 2018-19 announcement of fixing MSP at a level of at least 1.5 times the all-India weighted average cost of production.

title: NDTV Exclusive: How Mediator Pakistan Has Been Harbouring Iranian Military Jets For Years
content: In a statement, the government of Pakistan, while not denying the presence of the aircraft, has stated that it 'categorically rejects the CBS News report regarding the presence of Iranian aircraft at Nur Khan Air Base as misleading and sensationalise

title: PhD Student Of BITS Pilani Goa Dies By Suicide, 7th Case In 18 Months
content: Arjun Sabu, a second-year PhD student at the institute, was found hanging late Tuesday night, police said.

title: Global South Rising, BRICS Not Mere Symbolism: South Africa Minister To NDTV
content: On the eve of the BRICS Foreign Ministers' meeting in New Delhi, South Africa's Minister of International Relations and Cooperation, Ronald Lamola, delivered a sharp and confident message to the world - the Global South is rising.

title: Fatal Gujarat Road Pit Accidents Trigger Police Action Against Contractor
content: The Khavda Police have taken legal action by filing an FIR against a construction contractor following a series of fatal accidents on the Daddar-Sadhara road in Bhuj.

title: Saleswoman Caught On Camera Emptying Jewellery Trays Worth Rs 1.5 Crore
content: The woman, who was working as a saleswoman at a well-known jewellery store in the city, had a smile throughout while she cleared out the jewels from the store, showed the video.

title: Govt. revives annual journalism awards in Andhra Pradesh
content: Four-member committee constituted to oversee the selection and presentation of awards for the year 2025-26, says C.R. Media Academy Chairman Alapati Suresh Kumar

title: Security stepped up for visit of Chandrababu Naidu, Rajnath Singh to Puttaparthi on May 15
content: The leaders are scheduled to lay foundation stone for the ₹1 lakh-crore defence manufacturing facility that is expected to emerge as a major aerospace hub in the region and generate large-scale employment opportunities

title: Congress unveils ‘Bhu Guarantee’
content: The announcements include a reduction in the guidance value cost that needs to be paid for conversion from B-Khata to A-Khata, and regularisation of buildings in BDA areas constructed before 2008

title: Jamiat Ulama Society pays fine on behalf of prisoner for his release
content: Ramesh S. Kalagurthi, who was serving his sentence at the prison, was unable to clear ₹18,000 fine amount due to severe financial hardship

title: Shafi Parambil in crossfire as Kerala Chief Minister impasse triggers factional fault lines in Congress
content: Parambil has come under criticism on social media, particularly from sections in Malabar, over his perceived support for K.C. Venugopal as a chief ministerial contender

title: Amul, Mother Dairy hike milk prices by ₹2 per litre across India from May 14
content: It also stated that member unions have increased the procurement price paid to farmers by ₹30 per kg fat, marking a 3.7% rise compared to May 2025

title: ST quota back at 3% raises concerns over  proportional representation
content: While the 1931 census put ST population at about 0.8%, with more tribes added to the list, it is now estimated to be over 7% of Karnataka’s population

title: No talks were held with DMK, says Palaniswami
content: Countering rebel leader C.Ve. Shanmugam’s claim, the AIADMK general secretary alleges that the rebel MLAs, including some former Ministers, voted in favour of the TVK government’s motion of confidence, driven by “a desire for ministerial positions”

title: ‘Limited’ traditional and religious symbols to be allowed with prescribed uniforms in Karnataka schools and colleges
content: Under the fresh rules, students can wear turbans, sacred threads, rudraksha beads, Shiva beads, headscarves and similar traditional symbols along with the uniform

title: Watch: Gold and silver import duties doubled following Modi’s appeal | Above the Fold | 13.05.2026
content: In today’s episode Union government steps up austerity measures as the West Asia crisis deepens, with the Centre doubling gold and silver import duty and BJP leaders cutting convoy sizes, U.S. President Donald Trump’s China visit, and more.

title: Cyberabad police dispatch teams to Delhi, Karimnagar in hunt for Bandi Sanjay’s son in POCSO case
content: Bandi Sai Bageerath misses deadline to appear before investigators on Wednesday; protests erupt outside police station.

title: List on the bourses to thrive, IT Minister Lokesh tells MSMEs
content: Stating that the government is working in a mission mode to create 20 lakh jobs in both the public and private sectors in five years and restore ‘Brand A.P.’, the IT Minister says the MSMEs play a major supportive role in achieving it

title: In 2024, NTA and Govt. did not want to cancel NEET, cited student welfare in Supreme Court
content: Two years ago, the government had argued that cancelling the exam would be like treating “unequals equally” and that innocents and wrongdoers would be made to suffer same consequences

title: Cong. high command defers announcement of Kerala CM designate to Thursday
content: High command asks Congress MLAs-elect  to report to Indira Bhavan in Thiruvananthapuram, even as Congress workers congregate in front of the houses of Venugopal, Chennithala and Satheesan in anticipation of the momentous announcement from New Delhi

title: Policeman, five others die in accident in Tiruppur district
content: The policeman, along with a lorry driver, was rearranging the barricades on the road when a multi-purpose vehicle hit them from behind

title: Godavari Pushkarams 2027: CUMTA’s report on crowd management likely by June
content: At least 17 entry routes have been proposed for the devotees and their vehicles along the banks of river Godavari

title: Row over P. Jayarajan’s Kodungallur temple visit continues; BJP criticism meets researcher’s clarification
content: BJP’s B. Gopalakrishnan questions the visit, while researcher Vinil Paul says it was part of a larger field study on Kerala’s socio-religious history

title: Chandrababu Naidu bats for data-driven governance to build robust economy
content: The Gross State Domestic Product estimates should no longer rely solely on conventional quarterly and annual assessment methods, he says, and calls for adopting real-time monitoring systems capable of tracking economic activity every month across districts and mandals

title: Cabinet approves ₹20,667 crore for semi high-speed rail project connecting Ahmedabad-Dholera
content: As India’s first semi high-speed rail project using indigenous technology, it will act as a reference model for such projects across the country, Rail officials say; better connectivity for 284 villages

title: Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu sees need to adopt advanced tools to detect, prevent cyber frauds in Andhra Pradesh
content: Calling for an action plan to check financial crimes, the Chief Minister says there is a need for faster intervention systems capable of tracing and recovering the money lost during the crucial ‘golden hour’ immediately after a complaint is lodged

title: Mango and seasonal forest produce festival in Belagavi from tomorrow
content: The festival aims to celebrate and promote local mango varieties, seasonal forest produce (Ranmeva) and sustainable living practices

title: SRIVANI darshan ticket portal is fully secure, says TTD
content: Asserting that software architecture is continuously upgraded to strengthen security safeguards and prevent misuse of the system, TTD urges devotees not to fall prey to misinformation and unscrupulous middlemen

title: Two sub-registrars likely to be suspended as dead man signs not one but two land deals in Belagavi district
content: Two land deals, worth crores of rupees, were registered in the name of Digambar Shripad Kulkarni who had passed away on April 9, 1987

title: Congress and DMK allies reaffirm support to TVK government
content: When the TVK was 10 seats short of an absolute majority, the Congress, Left parties, the IUML, and the VCK decided to support it in forming a government. The aim was to prevent the BJP’s indirect rule, says Congress Legislature Party leader S. Rajesh Kumar

title: Kuppam aims for 10 lakh litres daily milk production by 2028
content: Collector urges officials to focus on fodder development through community-based programmes to improve feed availability for livestock during a meeting on development of MSMEs at Kuppam

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly: From ‘deep discussions’ among rebel AIADMK leaders during Palaniswami’s speech to AMMK MLA heaping praises on CM Vijay
content: BJP’s lone MLA in Tamil Nadu Assembly says session has become like a ‘campaign meeting’ against the BJP; , among others in today’s Assembly proceedings; PMK MLA Sowmiya Anbumani said a sustained effort was needed to end alcoholism

title: UDF’s landslide victory in Idukki constituency shows shifting of political votes
content: The massive victory of the UDF candidate in the Idukki Assembly constituency indicates a significant return of Kerala Congress (M) votes to the UDF fold

title: Iran war shows military force alone cannot be solution to conflicts: T.S. Tirumurti
content: The former Ambassador and Permanent Representative of India to the United Nations was speaking at the inauguration of Diplomacy and Sustainability Dialogues 2026, jointly organised by Saveetha Institute of Medical and Technical Sciences (SIMATS) and The Hindu

title: Govt. approves incentive for coal gasification projects with outlay of ₹37,500 crore
content: "We all know about the current geopolitical situation. So we have to take all the decisions to become ‘Atma Nirbhar’. In this context, a big decision on coal gasification was taken today," he said

title: The Dragon & the Dealmaker: Tariff warrior Trump arrives in China as trade pilgrim
content: the visit comes wrapped in imperial pomp and choreographed grandeur . under the ceremony lies a more transactional reality: Trump is effectively in China hunting for a trade deal that can ease economic anxieties at home . the symbolism of the trip is striking .

title: Watch: Gunshots heard inside Philippine Senate, lawmakers run for cover
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. content: At TOI Sports Desk, reporters work around the clock to bring you comprehensive updates from the world of sports. title: Amul raises milk prices by Rs 2 per litre across India from May 14
content: the TOI Business Desk is a vigilant and dedicated team of journalists committed to delivering the latest and most relevant business news from around the world to readers of the Times of India . the desk ensures that TOI readers are well-informed about the ever-changing and dynamic world of commerce .

title: Why 'Congress-mukt Bharat' may never become a reality
content: Why 'Congress-mukt Bharat' may not become reality Priyanka Mukherjee is an Assistant News Editor at The Times of India, where deadlines rarely slow down. Often juggling breaking news, long-form explainers and the everyday chaos of the newsroom, she is usually managing stress, chasing clarity and trying to log off on time (rarely successful). She also cares deeply about stories around women’s rights and social change, while two very opinionated dogs make sure she never truly switches off. Read More
